chapter_title.ar: باب الْيَمِينِ الَّتِي تَلْزَمُ صَاحِبَهَا الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 12 – The oath which necessitates the expiation upon its swearer

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/325)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/325)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْيَمِينُ الَّتِي تَلْزَمُنِي فِيهَا الْكَفَّارَةُ فَقَالَا مَا حَلَفْتَ عَلَيْهِ مِمَّا لِلَّهِ فِيهِ طَاعَةٌ أَنْ تَفْعَلَهُ فَلَمْ تَفْعَلْهُ فَعَلَيْكَ فِيهِ الْكَفَّارَةُ وَ مَا حَلَفْتَ عَلَيْهِ مِمَّا لِلَّهِ فِيهِ الْمَعْصِيَةُ فَكَفَّارَتُهُ تَرْكُهُ وَ مَا لَمْ يَكُنْ فِيهِ مَعْصِيَةٌ وَ لَا طَاعَةٌ فَلَيْسَ هُوَ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Sa’ad Bin Sa’ad, from Muhammad Bin Al Qasim Bin Al Fazl, from Hamza Bin Humran, from Dawood Bin Farqad, from Humran who said,

‘I said to Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> and Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The oath which necessitates the expiation upon me’. So they both said: ‘Whatever you have sworn upon (if it is) from what is obedience for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> therein if you were to do it, so you do not do it, then upon you is the expiation with regards to it; and whatever you swear upon (if it is) from what is the disobedience for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> therein, so its expiation is that you leave it; and whatever does not happen to have in it a disobedience, nor an obedience, so it is not with anything’.
en.sarwar: 3. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Sa‘d ibn Sa‘d from Muhammad ibn al-Qasim ibn al-Fudayl from Hamzah ibn Humran from Dawud ibn Farqad from Humran who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, and abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, about the kind of oath for which I owe expiation. They said, ‘It is when you take an oath for an act in which there is obedience to Allah if you do it and you do not do it, then there is expiation on you. If you take an oath for an act in which there is disobedience to Allah then its expiation is not to do it. An oath about the act which is not an obedience or disobedience does not have any valid effect.’”
